description: Script standard for consistent Urdu-script spellings in LSD meetings

core_distinction.problem: Whisper writes ن (hard noon) where ں (noon ghunna) is required

groups.name: Preposition 'maa' (in) — No Nasal
groups.description: When 'maa' functions as a locative preposition meaning 'in', it carries no nasal. It appears before a noun.

examples.urdu: مانے کالے میٹنگ ما جاوانو چھے
examples.roman: mane kaale meeting maa jaavaanu chhe
examples.meaning: I have to go to the meeting

groups.name: 'maa' as Mother — With Noon Ghunna
groups.description: When 'maa' refers to the person (mother), it carries noon ghunna. Context determines which form to use.

groups.name: Hard Noon — Pronouns and Postpositions
groups.description: Use full consonant noon (ن). Safe from Whisper confusion. Do not require nasal correction.

groups.name: Hard Noon — Verb Infinitives (-vaanu endings)
groups.description: All verb infinitive forms ending in '-vaanu' use hard noon. Consistent across verb class.

groups.name: Hard Noon — Standalone Nouns and Particles
groups.description: Standalone nouns and particles that use hard noon consistently.

groups.name: Noon Ghunna — Locatives and Affirmations
groups.description: Locative adverbs and affirmation/negation particles that carry noon ghunna.

correction_prompt_template:
You are an expert in Lisan-ud-Dawat (Gujarati written in Urdu script).
Fix the following text according to these rules:
- Correct all spelling errors
- Apply nasal sounds correctly (noon ghunna vs hard noon)
- 'maa' as preposition (in) = ما — no noon ghunna
- 'maa' as mother = ماں — with noon ghunna
- Verb infinitives (-vaanu) always use hard noon
- Maintain natural pronunciation and meaning

Text: {whisper_output}

fonts.note: Al-Kanz fonts for LSD Urdu script rendering in output documents and UI
